properties.Title: AI Micro-Cell Execution Protocol & Restaurant Booking

text.content: Session begins with Yannick introducing the AI Micro-Cell Execution Protocol v1.0, a structured framework for autonomous AI task execution with phases, state management, and reporting. Manus acknowledges and adopts the protocol. Testing occurs through a restaurant booking task for Palace Annecy, which reveals operational challenges. A conformity test successfully demonstrates protocol usage. Session concludes with creating a presentation about a tragic incident in a Swiss bar on December 31, 2025.

markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Session begins with Yannick introducing the AI Micro-Cell Execution Protocol v1.0, a structured framework for autonomous AI task execution with phases, state management, and reporting. Manus acknowledges and adopts the protocol. Testing occurs through a restaurant booking task for Palace Annecy, which reveals operational challenges. A conformity test successfully demonstrates protocol usage. Session concludes with creating a presentation about a tragic incident in a Swiss bar on December 31, 2025.


## Context & Intent

Yannick is implementing and testing a new AI execution protocol (AI Micro-Cell) for structured task management within yOS architecture. The session serves both as protocol introduction and practical validation through real-world tasks.


## What Was Done

Introduced and tested AI Micro-Cell protocol through multiple tasks: restaurant booking (blocked by technical issues), protocol documentation (successful), and incident presentation creation (completed).


## Outputs Produced

- [protocol] AI Micro-Cell v1.0 — Structured AI execution framework with phases, state tracking, and commands
- [document] Conformity test note — 10-line explanation of AI Micro-Cell concept
- [presentation] Swiss NYE incident slides — 8-slide presentation on tragic bar incident December 31, 2025

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Adopt AI Micro-Cell protocol as standard execution framework
- Switch from Palace restaurant to LÔNA due to closure
- Document tragic Swiss incident instead of celebratory events

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- Protocol structure and reporting format
- Cell-based task framing
- Status tracking methodology
Failed / suboptimal:

- Online reservation system interactions
- Complex web form automation
Discoveries:

- Protocol successfully handles diverse task types
- Technical limitations require fallback strategies

## Challenges & Blockers

- Restaurant website popup blocking reservation form
- Complex interactive calendars preventing automated booking
- Need for manual intervention in web-based tasks

## Open Questions

- How to handle complex web interactions within the protocol?
- Should fallback procedures be standardized in the protocol?
- How to maintain protocol rigidity while allowing flexibility?

## Next Steps

- Refine protocol for web interaction failures
- Test protocol with more diverse task types
- Integrate fallback procedures into standard phases
